title: Decide fixed-point versus floating-point datapath
description: Prototype the chain in floating point, then move to fixed point only where power or area demands it and the error budget allows.
body: Use when targeting an embedded DSP or FPGA where floating-point units are scarce. Do not begin in fixed point, because debugging quantization and algorithm bugs together obscures both. Actions: build the floating reference first, inspect the dynamic range at each stage, then assign integer word lengths from the measured headroom. Validation signals include the fixed-point output tracking the float reference within the budgeted SNR and no saturation events in a long stimulus run. Risk boundary: if measured quantization noise approaches the signal of interest, escalate to a wider word length rather than accepting silent degradation.
